nodes.label: Direction readouts — the spine inside the namer
nodes.rationale: Correction note: an earlier draft claimed cosine/NCM/Deep-SLDA all read class direction — two critics flagged that false (a distance to a prototype is a magnitude); this version is precise about which readout is actually spine-clean.

nodes.label: Cosine classifier — direction-pure
nodes.rationale: L2-normalize features AND class weights, classify by angle — the only strictly spine-clean readout; a cosine margin (angle to nearest vs runner-up) is the corrected north-star 'feeling' signal replacing the earlier (magnitude-based) SLDA-distance proposal.

nodes.label: NCM / Deep SLDA — magnitude-based but recency-robust
nodes.rationale: NCM = Euclidean distance to class mean; Deep SLDA = Mahalanobis distance with tied covariance, algebraically a linear-softmax with a ||mu||^2-growing per-class bias — both are magnitudes, but dodge Thread-1's recency bias because they have no trained softmax weights to inflate; recency-robust is not the same as direction-reading.

nodes.label: Magnitude-bias fixes (BiC / Weight Alignment / logit-adjust / SCR)
nodes.rationale: In class-incremental learning a plain linear classifier develops recency/high-norm bias; BiC rescales biased logits, Weight Alignment aligns weight norms, logit-adjusted online CL subtracts a per-class offset during streaming, SCR replaces softmax FC with NCM — the field independently re-derived 'trained-softmax magnitude is the continual failure mode.'

nodes.label: RanPAC — random projection + ridge prototype (near-twin)
nodes.rationale: Frozen features -> frozen nonlinear random projection -> ridge-regularized Gram-matrix class-prototype head, rehearsal-free SOTA, no gradient at all; the ridge (second-moment-aware) prototype beats plain NCM — a running mean+Gram are substrate-native statistics (cap EMA + running second moment).

nodes.label: Four-way readout bake-off (linear-softmax vs cosine vs NCM vs ridge-prototype)
nodes.rationale: Score on accuracy x BWT x spine-cleanliness (does the readout's verdict move under feature/prototype norm rescaling?); the sharp falsifiable claim: does cosine match the recency-robustness of magnitude-carrying NCM/ridge-prototype, or do we pay a forgetting cost to stay spine-clean.
